Empires & Puzzles Alvar Review: The Stag Family's Status Effect Sorcerer

Alvar isn't just a hero; he's a chaotic mailman who takes all your team's nasty ailments and delivers them straight to your enemies' doorstep. With a kit that resets durations and reflects buffs, he turns the tide of battle by reversing roles. But can his 2024 stats survive the hits of 2026? Let's find out.
Alvar: The "Return to Sender" Stag Sorcerer
Alvar is a 5-star Legendary Holy hero from the

Alliance Quest (Stag Family). Released back in August 2024, he brought a refreshing and slightly chaotic mechanic to the table: "Lifting" ailments. While most cleansers just wash away the dirt, Alvar picks it up and throws it in the neighbor's face. He is primarily a PvP offense hero designed to punish ailment-heavy defenses and disrupt buff-heavy teams. If you've ever wanted to give a taste of their own medicine to a team full of DoT casters, Alvar is your guy.

Alvar sports a whimsical, almost bard-like aesthetic that stands out from the gritty, armored knights usually found in the Clash of Knights event. He’s holding a scroll (presumably the "List of People Who Are About to Have a Bad Time") and blowing a horn. The art screams "messenger," but the message is definitely "I found this poison, and I think it belongs to you." It’s a lighter, more mischievous design that fits his trickster-like skill set perfectly.
Looking at Alvar's stats through the lens of early 2026, we have to be realistic:
Respectable. While not hitting the 1350+ highs of modern damage dealers, it's enough to make his 350% hit sting.
Below average. He is becoming quite fragile.

This is the danger zone. Modern heroes are pushing 2300+ base HP. Alvar's health pool is significantly smaller, making him a "glass cannon" utility hero. He risks dying before he can cast his average-speed skill if not heavily protected or emblemed for survival.
Special Skill: Scroll of New Beginnings
Alvar’s special skill is a multi-layered bag of tricks. Let’s break it down:
cleansable ailments from your team and randomly tosses them onto the enemy.

"Lifting" counts as removing, but it can trigger secondary effects of certain ailments (like Pepperflame’s or Pophit’s secondary damage) because the ailment is technically "ending" on your hero. Be careful!
This is the sleeper OP part of his kit. When he throws those ailments (or if enemies already have ailments), their duration resets to the
You cast a 3-turn burn. 2 turns pass. Alvar fires. That burn is now back to 3 turns (or whatever the max is). Infinite suffering!
350% to all enemies is a solid slap. It’s not a team-wipe on its own in the current era of massive HP pools, but it softens them up significantly.
For 4 turns, if an enemy tries to cast a buff, it gets reflected to a random member of
team. This completely shuts down buffer-heavy defenses.
With a Level 23 Mana Troop or Level 11 Magic/Styx Troop, you shave off 1 tile (9 tiles).
To get to 9 tiles, you need a total of +12% mana generation. His class (Sorcerer) grants +4% at node 20. You need +8% from troops/bards.
raw damage before defense reduction. Against modern defense (~1300-1500), expect hits in the 600-800 range per enemy. It’s "softening" damage, not "finishing" damage.
5% heal per buff. This is his only sustain. In a buff-heavy team (or when stealing buffs), this can add up to 20-30% healing over a match, which helps offset his low base HP.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

bonus is fantastic for him. If he receives an Attack Down ailment, it flips into an Attack Up buff (+40%/+60%/+70%). This makes him naturally resistant to attack-droppers, ensuring his 350% hit keeps its potency.
As mentioned, recovering 5% HP every time he gets a buff or stack is crucial for his survival. Pair him with spammers like Faez or heavy buffers to keep his health topped up.

At the start of battle, he gets +20% defense for 6 turns. This is arguably the best Aether Power for him because it patches his biggest weakness (squishiness) during the critical opening phase before he can fire.
Upon release in 2024, Alvar was seen as a "fun but tricky" hero. He wasn't the meta-defining tank that some sought, but offensive players loved the ability to weaponize enemy ailments.
The "Lift" mechanic is timeless—utility doesn't age as badly as damage numbers. However, his stats
aged. 1730 HP is low. He dies easily to the likes of modern snipers who deal 600%+ damage.
He is still excellent in specific War attacks (e.g., against heavy DoT teams) or Rush Tournaments. He is
a plug-and-play hero for defense anymore; he's too fragile.
Alvar is a Sorcerer. We strongly recommend prioritizing survival to ensure he lives long enough to cast his Average speed skill.
Maximizes his lifespan. Pushes his defense closer to modern standards and pads that scary low HP stat.
His 350% hit will be slightly less impactful (but a dead hero deals 0 damage).
Maximizes the AoE damage. Useful if you run him with a taunt hero who guarantees his safety.
He will likely die to a stiff breeze or slash attacks before firing.
path (Defense/Health) is recommended. His utility (reset/reflect/lift) is his main selling point, not his raw damage.
Turns the enemy's strength (DoT, blinds, defense down) against them.
The reflect mechanic forces the enemy to buff
Pairs beautifully with other DoT heroes due to the duration reset.
Passive healing on buffs helps him stay in the fight.
He is statistically fragile compared to 2025/2026 heroes.
In a fast meta, he might be too slow without mana support.
"Lifting" ailments can trigger their "on removal" damage (e.g., Pepperflame), which might kill your team if you aren't careful.
AI controls him poorly, and he's too squishy to hold the line.
Excellent counter-pick against DoT wings or slower defenses.
At Very Fast speed, he becomes a nightmare, constantly resetting ailments and reflecting buffs.
Ailment redirection is powerful here since cleansing is rare/limited, and he adds damage.
Jwala (or any fast burner) - Alvar - Healer - Support - Sniper
Fire your burner first. Let the burn tick. Fire Alvar to reset the burn duration to full and spread any other nastiness.
Use a Taunt hero to protect Alvar while he charges. Use Goldie/Faez to spam buffs, triggering Alvar's passive heal constantly.
If enemies have immunity, Alvar can't stick the lifted ailments on them.
Be careful lifting ailments that deal massive damage upon removal if your team is low on health.
If you pulled Alvar, congrats! He is a fun, manual-play hero. Focus on keeping him alive. Don't put him on defense; use him in Raids when you see an enemy team that relies on poison or fire.

Alvar is a "luxury tool." He's not a daily driver. He shines in deep War rosters where you need a specific counter to ailment-heavy teams. Do not Limit Break 2 him unless you absolutely love his playstyle; those Alpha Aethers are better spent on modern stats.
There was significant confusion on the forums about whether "Lifting" triggers secondary damage effects like Pepperflame's.
It does. As one user noted,
"My testing shows the lifting does not trigger the secondary effect"
—which was quickly debunked by others sharing screenshots of massive damage spikes.
One player hilariously remarked, *"Sounds interesting. Like a updated version of my favorite puppy Ferdinand."
A popular YouTuber titled their review
"Time to give FS heroes the Whale Finger,"
praising Alvar's ability to punish passive defenses, though noting he requires high-level play to work well.

Alvar is the definition of a "high risk, high reward" hero. His stats are starting to show their age, but his kit remains one of the most unique and disruptive in the game. He allows you to play ping-pong with status effects, and frankly, winning by killing an enemy with their own poison is unmatched satisfaction.
Keep him for offense, protect him with your life, and enjoy the chaos.
